title: Today's AI Brief: AI Agents Plunged the Tech World Into Chaos. Here’s Exactly How That Happened
summary: AI agents cause chaos in tech, Google's new AI model impresses, and AI addiction takes center stage.

imageSourceTitle: AI Agents Plunged the Tech World Into Chaos. Here’s Exactly How That Happened

body.text: I'm looking at some pretty wild developments in the world of AI. It seems like AI agents have plunged the tech world into chaos. I'm not talking about your run-of-the-mill glitch, but full-blown, can't-even-pretend-it's-not-a-thing chaos. Let's dive into a couple of stories that caught my eye.

First up, there's a group called Claude Code Anonymous. Now, I know what you're thinking - another anonymous group online. But hear me out. This one's for people addicted to using AI tools, specifically something called Claude. I guess you could say it's like a support group, but instead of 12 steps, they've got 12 prompts or something. The point is, AI use has gotten to a point where people are gathering in secret to discuss their struggles. That's some next-level stuff right there.

I'm not sure what's more surprising - that people are struggling with AI addiction or that they're being open about it. Either way, it's clear that AI is becoming a bigger part of our lives, and we need to figure out how to navigate that.

AI Agents Plunged the Tech World Into Chaos. Here’s Exactly How That Happened is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: “Hi, my name is Peter, and I’m a Claudeholic.” It was August 2025 and Peter Steinberger was addressing a meetup in London called Claude Code Anonymous. Steinberger and some fellow addicts had arranged the event to network with people like themselves—techies swept up by coding tools such as Anthropic ’s paradigm-busting Claude Code. “I dedicate pretty much a... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

Google’s new anything-to-anything AI model is wild, from theverge.com, is also worth a quick mention. The main content says: Tech AI Google Google’s new anything-to-anything AI model is wild Omni sent my kid’s stuffie rafting and deepfaked me in front of the Eiffel Tower. But it’s not quite the singularity. by Allison Johnson May 23, 2026, 11:00 AM UTC Just a stuffed deer having the time of his life. | Image: Gemini / The Verge Part Of Google I/O 2026: All the news and announceme... The point is not to force it into a giant theory; it is simply part of today's news mix.

My quick read is that today's set of stories is useful because it gives us texture, not a sermon. Each item has its own little job: one tells us what changed, another tells us what people are worried about, and another gives the day its mood.
